what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard block that records a design argument rejecting member-gated data access, three-channel convergence, and two-engine convergence as prerequisites for a loop involving Tim, and recommending that the work be deferred.
observations: Title is prefixed 'P11 · ❌' and references BD-E, BD-K, and design-map B3/B4
Frontmatter declares type 'block', source 'claude_code', state 'current', channel 'dragnet-development', and a 'part_of' link to board://item-f9f4a6a6
Created and updated timestamps are identical, with a single 'filed' history entry
Body claims the design map says membership gates delivery, not access, and that access-gating does not exist
It states there are three 'channel' systems and that the brief treats convergence as a prerequisite
It argues Tim's default is 'every member can do everything', which is already the current free reality
It notes there is exactly one human (Tim) and a lead that already routes comments
It recommends posting to the existing comments channel and deferring access-control/channel-convergence until a second principal must be walled off
standouts: Explicit rejection of a stated prerequisite using a ❌ marker and P11 ID
Deferral rationale tied to a single-principal current reality rather than technical impossibility
References specific design-map cells and engine/channel codes without defining them in the file
concerns: Assumes Tim's loop will remain single-principal without stating why that is safe
Uses strong prescriptive language whose correctness depends on external context not present in the file
Does not include or quote the design-map content it interprets
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history
Uses board:// URI for linked items
Markdown body with a level-3 heading and bold emphasis for key claims
P## numbering with status emoji in the title
notes: A noticeboard item pushing back on member-gated access and channel/engine convergence as prerequisites, urging deferral until a second walled-off principal exists.
questions: What exactly are BD-E, BD-K, and design-map cells B3/B4?
Who is Tim and what is 'Tim's loop'?
What are the three channel systems and the two engines mentioned?
What is the lead's current routing mechanism and how does it replace access control?
What does 'membership gates delivery, not access' mean in the design map?
purpose_vs_actual: matches — the file argues and records the deferral position described in its title and body
apparent_themes: fidelity to a design map over a brief
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to prevent scope creep by challenging a prerequisite, keeping Tim's loop unblocked, and aligning work with the current single-principal reality rather than future permissions architecture.
novelty: It uses a ❌ marker and P11 ID to record an explicit pushback against a brief prerequisite, grounding the argument in the absence of a second principal rather than in technical feasibility.
contribution: It captures a contextual argument for deferring multi-tenant access control and channel/engine convergence, preserving the rationale that current membership defaults already suffice for a single principal.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard block item-1ee74515 records a rebuttal that member-gated data access, three-channel convergence, and two-engine convergence are not prerequisites for Tim's loop and should be deferred until a second isolated principal exists.
intention_for_embedding: To block premature access-control and convergence work by arguing the current single-principal, member-can-do-everything reality makes it unnecessary now.
